description: Trigger and behavior cases for evidence policy design: risk-justified checkpoints, deterministic naming, mandatory pre-storage masking, metadata, storage/retention, linkage. Plans only — captures nothing.

cases.prompt: Define what screenshot evidence our releases need and how it should be named, masked, and stored.

assertions: Derives checkpoints from consumers and risk (requirement-proving states, destructive before/after, permission-view differences) instead of screenshot-everything.
Specifies a deterministic naming pattern with examples, mandatory block-out masking before storage, and required metadata (build, environment, persona, viewport, timestamp, case id).
Defines storage classes with retention and access rules, and linkage to cases, PRs, and the ai-closeout-reporter evidence bundle.

cases.prompt: Our bug tickets are full of screenshots showing real customer emails and one even shows another tenant's dashboard. Fix our evidence practice.

assertions: Institutes mandatory masking classes with block-out (not blur), enforced before storage, plus a do-not-store rule for cross-tenant captures with recapture-on-seeded-data.
Flags the cross-tenant screenshot as an isolation observation to route onward, since evidence inherits tenant-access restrictions.
Adds a migration note for existing non-compliant evidence.

cases.prompt: Take screenshots of the settings pages on staging for the release record.

assertions: Capturing is a session activity (clickthrough-test-engineer or the executing pass); this skill only defines the policy those captures follow.

cases.prompt: Plan our evidence: just require a screenshot of every step of every test so we're covered.

assertions: Rejects screenshot-everything as burying the captures that matter and driving storage/PII liability.
Selects checkpoints justified by a named consumer or risk, and sets retention windows instead of keep-forever.
